method: LINE-ISOLATION permutation test (L2 token-position structure). Metric: fraction of target tokens (anonymous TYPE by raw substring '\U0001076b') whose immediately-preceding token-type is in {nl,div,START} AND immediately-following token-type is in {nl,div,END}. Null: within each inscription, re-place the same number of target tokens uniformly at random among the inter-content-token gaps of the non-target skeleton (preserving nl structure + target count); >=500/2000 reshuffles; perm p = frac(null>=obs), one-sided enrichment. Positive control synthetic (DETECT always-isolated; FALSE-POSITIVE uniform-random). Cross-site per site with >=15 targets; leave-one-site-out on Haghia Triada.
key_findings: The anonymous token TYPE whose raw contains U+1076B is line-isolated in 432/474 = 91.1% of occurrences, vs a within-inscription uniform re-placement null of 22.3% (perm p=0.0005, 2000 reshuffles) — a ~4x enrichment far beyond position-shuffle chance.

Flanking structure is dominated by line/document boundaries: preceding token is nl/START in 429/474 cases (90.5%) and following token is nl/END in 448/474 (94.5%); content tokens (word/num/other) flank on at least one side in only ~9% of cases.

The effect is CROSS-SITE: all 5 sites with >=15 target tokens (Haghia Triada 94.7%, Khania 92.9%, Zakros 96.2%, Knossos 57.1%, Phaistos 90.9%) are individually significant and enriched (perm p=0.0005 each), so it is not an HT-only artefact.

Leave-one-site-out dropping Haghia Triada (the largest site) leaves the global signal intact: obs=88.9% vs null=23.6%, p=0.0005 over 287 targets — the marker behaviour survives removal of the dominant site.

Positive control (synthetic) PASSED: the test flags a planted always-isolated corpus (detect p=0.002) and rejects a uniform-random-placement corpus in 0/25 draws (false-positive rate 0.0 <= 0.10), so the machinery is calibrated and the enrichment is not a null-model artefact.
successor_hypotheses: E062: Does the U+1076B line-isolated marker preferentially occupy the FIRST or LAST content-line of an inscription (heading/colophon position) rather than interior lines? (L2 position-in-document)
E063: Is the marker's per-line frequency capped at 1 (one marker per content-line), or can multiple markers share a line — testing separator vs heading semantics? (L2)
E064: Do lines immediately adjacent to the marker carry systematically different token-type compositions (e.g. more num, more div) than non-adjacent lines? (L2)
E065: Are there OTHER frequent 'other' token TYPEs (beyond U+1076B) that show comparable line-isolation, suggesting a CLASS of structural markers? (L2, comparative)

E066: Does the marker co-occur with inscription-level metadata fields (support=Tablet vs other, context LMIB) at above-chance rates — i.e. is it a document-class indicator? (L2/L3 boundary, metadata only)
E067: Within multi-line inscriptions, does the marker predict the start of a new 'section' as defined by a div token or a numeric cluster on the following line? (L2)
non_circular: Target = anonymous token TYPE selected purely by raw substring match ('\U0001076b' in raw) on the 'other' token class; metric = pure token-position structure (line-isolation by adjacent token-type); null = within-inscription uniform re-placement among skeleton gaps preserving nl structure and target count. No sign value, reading, phonetics, or meaning used at any step. Positive control is synthetic and declared. Layer L2 only.
